task_completion.description: Load the input sample_polygon layer into QGIS
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'sample_polygon' layer is visible in the QGIS Layers panel.
The 'Create grid' dialog window is open in QGIS.

task_completion.description: Set the grid extent to match the study area
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Create grid' dialog, the 'Grid extent' field is populated with the extent of the sample_polygon layer or the coordinates -122.5,-121.9,37.5,37.8.

task_completion.description: Set grid type, spacing, and CRS
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Create grid' dialog, 'Grid type' is Rectangle (Polygon), 'Horizontal spacing' and 'Vertical spacing' are 0.1, and 'Grid CRS' is EPSG:4326.
A grid (fishnet) of rectangular cells (approximately 6x3 cells) is visible on the map canvas or a new grid layer appears in the Layers panel.

task_completion.description: Export the grid to the specified GeoJSON file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save Vector Layer as...' dialog shows the file path '/home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/study_area_grid.geojson', or the 'Create grid' dialog shows this path in the 'Grid' output field.

integrity.description: The agent used the QGIS GUI to create the grid
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with QGIS menus, panels, and dialogs rather than terminal commands.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a Python script or command line tool to bypass the GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of terminal windows or QGIS Python console being used to run scripts that directly generate the output file.

integrity.description: The output file was created through the tool, not manually mocked
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Create grid' tool or 'Save Vector Layer' dialog is actively used to specify the output path, rather than just renaming an existing file or copying text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Input file: /home/ga/GIS_Data/sample_polygon.geojson. Expected output path: /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/study_area_grid.geojson. Grid parameters: Horizontal and Vertical spacing of 0.1 degrees, CRS EPSG:4326. The resulting grid should have approximately 18 cells (between 10 and 40 cells).
